source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Following is the K-map of a Boolean function of five variables \( P, Q, R, S \), and \( X \). The minimum sum-of-products (SOP) expression for the function is:

choice_map.A: \( \bar{P} \bar{Q} S \bar{X} + P \bar{Q} S \bar{X} + Q \bar{R} \bar{S} X + Q R \bar{S} X \)

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'Following is the K-map', so the prompt depends on a diagram not fully encoded in text
image_manifest contains 1 existing PNG image with readable dimensions 384x384
multi_step: finding a minimum SOP from a five-variable K-map typically requires multiple operations: reading map cells/groups, forming implicants, and minimizing the expression
the task is not simple lookup; it involves translating visual groupings into Boolean terms
there are 4 answer choices, so the solver may also need to compare derived implicants against options
verifiability: answer_text is a specific symbolic Boolean expression, giving a clear target output
choices are provided and the answer matches a single option format, which supports straightforward checking
